title: Blue Origin completes investigation into New Glenn launch failure
summary: Blue Origin has concluded the investigation into the New Glenn rocket's third flight failure, allowing the vehicle to resume launches. The company has addressed the issues and is now cleared to proceed with future missions.

title: SpaceX launches first Starship V3
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the first Starship V3 on May 22, achieving most of the test objectives during the suborbital flight.

title: Space Force awards Viasat, SES $437 million for military satellite network
summary: The U.S. Space Force has awarded Viasat and SES a $437 million contract to build four small geostationary satellites for the Protected Tactical Satcom-Global program. This initiative aims to enhance secure military communications.

title: NASA unveils sweeping reorganization
summary: NASA announced a major reorganization on May 22, merging mission directorates and restructuring personnel. This marks one of the agency's most significant reorganizations in recent years.

title: Leveraging AUKUS and southern geography: building Australia’s dual-use space infrastructure for strategic resilience
summary: Australia is advancing its dual-use space infrastructure, with the Deep Space Advanced Radar Capability (DARC) Site 1 in Western Australia providing early tracking data for AUKUS partners. Full operational capability for the DARC site is targeted for 2027.

title: SpaceX IPO filing casts Starlink Mobile as future wireless challenger
summary: SpaceX's IPO filing highlights Starlink Mobile as a potential competitor to terrestrial wireless networks, positioning it to challenge traditional mobile services even in urban areas. The company aims to expand its direct-to-smartphone capabilities beyond remote regions.

title: Rocket Lab launches ninth Synspective satellite
summary: Rocket Lab successfully launched its ninth radar imaging satellite for Synspective. The company also plans to sell up to $3 billion in stock to fund future initiatives.

title: Rocket Lab wins first GEO satellite production contract from U.S. Space Force
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract from the U.S. Space Force to build and operate two GEO satellites equipped with optical payloads. This marks Rocket Lab's first GEO satellite production contract with the U.S. Space Force.

title: Ground equipment problem scrubs Starship launch attempt
summary: SpaceX postponed the inaugural launch of its latest Starship vehicle on May 21 due to a ground equipment issue. The launch attempt was scrubbed before liftoff.

title: Sixth Varda mission successfully returns
summary: Varda Space Industries successfully completed its sixth reentry mission on May 18, advancing pharmaceutical research and hypersonic testing. The company is balancing commercial and scientific objectives in its space missions.

title: China reveals 3-person Shenzhou 23 crew, including Hong Kong's 1st astronaut
summary: China has announced the three astronauts for the Shenzhou 23 mission, including the first astronaut from Hong Kong. The crew is set to launch on May 24.

title: 'Rick and Morty' Season 9: Release date & how to watch Adult Swim’s zany sci-fi satire
summary: Adult Swim announced the release date for 'Rick and Morty' Season 9, promising more zany sci-fi satire. The show's unique blend of humor and science fiction continues to captivate audiences.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 211 — Oysters in Space
summary: The latest episode of the 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with Jacob Scoccimarro of Monolith Space about space-related topics, including 'Oysters in Space'.

title: SpaceX Starship Flight 12: Don't miss these stunning photos from the launch of the most powerful Starship yet
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its 12th Starship flight, featuring the more powerful Starship V3, from Starbase. The launch provided stunning photography as part of efforts to eventually land astronauts on the moon.

title: 'For All Mankind's' Costa Ronin on ruling Mars, honoring cosmonaut history, and learning to tie his tie (interview)
summary: Costa Ronin discusses his role in 'For All Mankind' and the challenges of portraying leadership on Mars while honoring cosmonaut history. The interview also touches on personal anecdotes, including learning to tie a tie.

title: Massive 'X' and 'V' shapes will appear on the moon tonight — here's how to see them
summary: Massive 'X' and 'V' shapes will be visible on the moon during the first quarter phase. The phenomenon can be observed with a telescope or binoculars.

title: Watch China launch Shenzhou 23 astronauts to its Tiangong space station on May 24
summary: China is set to launch the Shenzhou 23 mission to its Tiangong space station on May 24, which will relieve an overdue crew and may mark the start of the country's first year-long stay in orbit.

title: SpaceX just launched Starship V3 — its most powerful megarocket yet — into space for the 1st time in spectacular Flight 12 test (video)
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Starship V3, the most powerful rocket ever built, for the first time in Flight 12 test. The event marked a significant milestone in spaceflight technology.

title: 'Once you try these, you won’t want anything else'— Our binoculars expert rated these  as the best small binoculars they'd ever tested. Now at their best price of the year
summary: A Nikon binoculars expert rated the Nikon 12x25 Binoculars as the best small binoculars, praising their image stabilization and portability for stargazing.

title: 'For All Mankind's' Ruby Cruz and Ines Asserson on wearing those 'clunky' cool spacesuits and training with a Green Beret (interview)
summary: Actors Ruby Cruz and Ines Asserson discuss the challenges of wearing realistic spacesuits and training with a Green Beret for the show 'For All Mankind'. The spacesuits were described as heavy and complex, reflecting real-world conditions.

title: Call for Creatives: NASA Seeks Help Illuminating Mission Storytelling
summary: NASA is seeking creative partners to help tell the stories of its missions, including Artemis Moon missions, nuclear propulsion, and aeronautics. The agency issued a call for proposals on May 21 for filmmakers, documentarians, and other storytellers.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Roscosmos Spacewalk Outside Space Station
summary: NASA will livestream a Roscosmos spacewalk outside the International Space Station on May 27, starting at 10:15 a.m. EDT and lasting five hours. Coverage begins at 9:45 a.m. on NASA+, Amazon Prime, and NASA’s YouTube channel.

title: Webb Studies Star Clusters
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope observed nearly 9,000 star clusters in four nearby galaxies, including Messier 51 (M51). The study reveals that more massive star clusters form more frequently.

title: Keeping NASA Flying: Ground Crews Ensure Aircraft Readiness
summary: NASA relies on ground crews at its Armstrong Flight Research Center to maintain aircraft for high-speed research and science missions. The center has recently added new aircraft to its fleet.

title: NASA to Compete Contract for Jet Propulsion Laboratory Management
summary: NASA plans to compete the next contract for managing the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL), currently operated by Caltech since its inception. The move aims to ensure accountability and maximize value for U.S. taxpayers.

title: NASA Announces Realignment to Accelerate Mission Delivery
summary: NASA announced an agencywide realignment to accelerate mission delivery and align with the National Space Policy. The changes aim to improve efficiency and focus on the nation's top priorities.

title: New Material Could Help NASA Melt Moon Rocks, Harness Lunar Resources
summary: NASA's Glenn Research Center discovered a material that could help melt Moon rocks to extract metals and oxygen, enabling astronauts to utilize lunar resources for future missions.

title: Hubble Captures Galaxy Cluster
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a galaxy cluster with galaxies of various shapes and sizes. The image also features foreground stars with distinctive diffraction spikes.

title: NASA to Showcase Mission to Boost Swift Spacecraft’s Orbit
summary: NASA will showcase a mission to boost the orbit of the Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory using Northrop Grumman’s Pegasus XL rocket and a Katalyst robotic spacecraft named LINK. The event will take place at NASA’s Wallops Flight Facility in Virginia on June 17.

title: Shenzhou 23 set to launch new crew to Tiangong space station
summary: China is preparing to launch the Shenzhou 23 mission to send a new crew to the Tiangong space station, which has been continuously occupied since June 2022. This marks another step in China's ongoing space station operations.

title: Starship Flight 12: Block 3 Impresses on First Flight
summary: Starship Flight 12, featuring Block 3, successfully launched on May 22, 2026, from Boca Chica, Texas, demonstrating significant improvements over previous flights.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled globally in the coming week, including SpaceX's 12th Starship flight, a Falcon 9 mission, an Electron launch, and a Vega C mission.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) launched the SMILE mission aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. This mission aims to investigate the interactions between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The Super Heavy Block 3 booster, part of SpaceX's Starship program, has undergone multiple iterations. This development highlights advancements in the booster's design and capabilities.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is actively processing and planning Artemis III, a significant crewed mission to the Moon. The mission includes a key Human Landing System (HLS) test.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company. The company's growth marked a significant milestone in its expansion.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now ready for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt. The mission follows the docking of Progress MS-34 to the station.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, but no specific details were provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant contributions by the European Space Agency.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and select materials for spacecraft to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section relies on innovative ideas from these young minds to support ESA's missions.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space-related activities.

title: Sensitive and sturdy
summary: The news discusses the durability and sensitivity of a space industry component or technology, though specific details are not provided.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving a parachute system, likely for a space mission. The 'bake-out' process suggests thermal testing to ensure the parachute's integrity under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of life while adhering to planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing and securing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: The US Needs a New Launch Site, Air Force Secretary Says
summary: The US Air Force Secretary has stated that current military launch sites are nearing maximum capacity, necessitating the development of a new launch site. This highlights the growing demand for space launch capabilities.

title: ESA Chief Calls on Europe to Double Funds for Space
summary: ESA's chief urges Europe to double funding for the space sector, citing significant progress but insufficient investment according to some industry leaders.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's recent stakeholder survey revealed that improved Moon technology is the top priority for space tech investments. Two years ago, NASA sought input from the space community to identify key technical challenges.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) has approved two new Scout science missions aimed at demonstrating cost-effective scientific research in space. These missions are designed to deliver significant scientific results with reduced budgets.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67B in revenue for 2025 ahead of its upcoming IPO, expected next month. The financial data was filed with the SEC.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could escalate the nuclear arms race rather than protect the U.S. The system is intended as a missile defense measure.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests that NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, potentially posing a security risk. The report highlights concerns over collaborations that could compromise U.S. interests.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving decision-making processes in engineering production, aiming to reduce inefficiencies and program stalls. The company is shifting from traditional design reviews to a more advanced 'decision intelligence' approach.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) warns that launch supply may fall short depending on the scenario, with potential annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, announced the launch of a new satellite bus business line. The initiative targets low-cost, high-volume, and high-power satellite missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security.

title: ST Engineering iDirect’s New CEO Kuppanna Talks Ground Tech’s Cloud-Native, Interoperable Future
summary: ST Engineering iDirect appointed Sridhar Kuppanna as its new CEO to lead the company's ground technology advancements amid a dynamic market. The move aims to address the evolving needs of cloud-native and interoperable solutions in the satellite communications sector.

title: UK Inaugurates New Space Domain Awareness Software Borealis
summary: The UK has launched a new space domain awareness software called Borealis, which is now operational six months ahead of schedule. The software enhances the tracking of satellites and space debris for the UK government.

title: Rocket Lab to Build First GEO Satellites for Space Force Space Domain Awareness Mission
summary: Rocket Lab has secured a $90 million contract to build the first Geostationary Orbit (GEO) satellites for a U.S. Space Force space domain awareness mission. This marks Rocket Lab's entry into GEO satellite production for national security applications.

title: Viasat Completes D2D Demo in Uzbekistan
summary: Viasat has successfully completed a direct-to-device (D2D) demonstration in Uzbekistan, showcasing messaging capabilities on Android smartphones via Viasat’s L-band satellite technology.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth funding round to support its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to accelerate its development and deployment efforts through this investment.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and develop innovative solutions to support satellite operations in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. The technology aims to enhance accessibility and usability of satellite imagery and analytics.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the first detailed look at the company's financials. This move offers unprecedented transparency into the financial health of the aerospace giant.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) raises concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence over critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to heavily impact the growing space sector.
